message:
fix(unix): return full-length initialized buffer from SocketAncillary::prepare_for_recv()

nix::recvmsg reads from the slice's length (not Vec capacity), so when
prepare_for_recv() returned `&mut Vec<u8>` after calling clear(), the
resulting empty slice meant recvmsg had zero space for ancillary data.

Changed the return type from `&mut Vec<u8>` to `&mut [u8]` and replaced
the clear() call with resize(capacity, 0) so the buffer is fully
initialized and exposed at its full length on every receive call.

Added test verifying the returned slice length matches the requested
capacity (128 bytes).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
